-- 1 of 4 --

Launch List Secrets, Part 3
!
Let's just go a little bit deeper on the launch list and list in general, and response rates
and that kind of thing.
!
When I first started, I was very lucky that some of the earliest teaching that I was
exposed to was about direct marketing and direct mail. Frankly, that's the only thing
that was available back then because no one was teaching anything about marketing
online or on the internet because there really wasn't any. No one had a clue. There was
just no knowledge base, so I studied a lot of direct marketing stuff.
!
Typical response rate, what I learned back then, typical response rate might be 1 to 2%
if you do some type of direct mail. That's pretty funny in itself because the difference
between 1% and 2% is just, it's huge, it's a double, right? I mean, if you send out a
thousand pieces and you get a 1% response, you're going to sell 10. If you got a 2%
response, you're going to sell 20. You double your business going from 1 to 2%, so it's
pretty crazy. It's a pretty big range.
But in any case, people wonder what's a typical response in launches?
!
I guess it’s sort of like that 1 to 2%. It's all over the place. It's impossible to answer. It
completely depends on your market and your price and your position in the market, and
your list, and your offer, and how you well you make that offer, your copy. There's
absolutely nothing typical, but generally I'm looking for better than 1 to 2%.
In niches, you can, especially if you have a small list and you have a warm relationship,
you can have way better than 1 to 2%. To show you the upper end, I actually did a
launch once where I had a tiny, slivered niche. It was a great, big niche but it was just
like a tiny, little sliver and I just had this whole list universe of 300 people. I had a very
warm relationship with those 300 people. It was sort of like the perfect storm in terms
of conversion. So, these 300 people and I had a very warm relationship.
!
I was trying to make the most difficult sale ever which was, I had been giving them this
service for free and now I wanted to charge them for it. There's no harder sale to make
than to say, "Hey, I know I've been giving this for free for like 3 years from me, but now
I want to charge you for it." Toughest sale in the world, but besides that, everything
else is perfect. It was a community. They were tied in the community. There were very
much tied into that. They loved me. I've been supporting them. I had this warm
relationship I could target. Since there's only 300 of them and I knew them, knew my
avatar really well, I could target the message to them really perfectly.
!
In any case, my response rate was 99%. I got 297 out of 300 to say, "Yes, I'll buy it
from you." I don't think I'm ever going to improve on a 99% response rate. Like I said,
it's not typical like a perfect storm. If I went out to the broader niche. There's no way I

-- 2 of 4 --

would have had any kind of conversion at all, but it was absolutely a perfect fit. It was
essentially like a new list that was carefully curetted. Again, there is absolutely nothing
typical but the way we will see the best conversion is if you carefully built a list over
some period of time and have a really warm relationship and they love you, and they're
just waiting to buy from you, and now you put together a launch that's perfectly fit for
them.
!
Just to throw out some other numbers, and this is completely untypical as well, but in
my Product Launch Formula launches, I've typically done about anywhere from $50 per
name on my email list up to a hundred dollars per name on my email list. Now
generally, it's somewhere in the 75 to $85 per name. Of course, measuring any of the
numbers in one of my launches can get messy because it's a Joint Venture launch.
!
We've got Joint Venture partners that are mailing at all different times into your launch.
Some of them are mailing right into the very beginning of your pre-launch and now
these people are on your list right from the start. They join your list right here at PLC1.
Other partners are mailing in PLC2. Other partners are mailing on your 3rd video. Some
of them are mailing at your sales video, some are not.
!
It's tough to measure but if you look at it, since my product, I don't want to get too
fancy on the math here, but since my product's selling for $2,000 at least right now, I
must have raised the price since you're watching it I've already raised the price. When I
was selling it for $2,000, $50 per name is basically 2½ % conversion, right? I don't
even want to get into the math, but $100 per name would be doing 5% conversion. I
hope I got that math right. I'm going to pull up my calculator after I shoot this video
and check it out, but I think I got that right. T
!
hat's the kind of conversion I'm getting on a very high ticket item, but that's because
I'm bringing them in. These are people that are joining my launch list. They're coming
warm because they're generally referred from a Joint Venture partner and so, there's
that endorsement. They give me a strong endorsement and you could see some really
seriously great numbers there. I mean, generating 75, $85 or more per name on your
email list is really powerful.
!
If I went out to my entire list as well, over a hundred thousand people now. Let's just
add another 0 in there. My list is over a hundred thousand people, but if I go and I
mailed that list directly, I am not going to get that $75 per name. I'm not even going to
get the $50 per name because they've been on my list for a while. They've seen my
stuff for a while. They're sick of me, right? That's how list are. They just stop paying
attention to you as closely. When I'm talking about that, 50, 75, $100 per name, it's the
launch list. It’s people that have just come into my list, are newly exposed to me,
whether they came from my old list or whether they came from my partner’s list.
One of the things that's important here is just like I threw all kinds of different numbers
at you from 99% conversion to $75 per name. It all varies by so many different factors.

-- 3 of 4 --

!
The three biggest factors is your offer, the copy for your offers, how great your
prelaunch is, and the list. The thing is, with the launch list, you're creating a great list.
If you get that piece out of the way and then it just comes down to your offer and your
copy. This is a key piece. This is really, really important. The better your math is, the
better your conversions, the more money you make for each person on your list, the
better off you do because that makes all the math work better.
!
If I can get $50, let's just say, again, to make the math easy, I've had a few launches
that were over a hundred dollars per name.
!
Let's say I do a launch where I generate a hundred dollars per name on my list. Now,
let's say I have partners and I'm asking those partners to mail into my pre-launch and if
I'm giving them a 50% conversion, that means, 50% of that is $50.00 bucks. Okay?
That means for everyone on average if my partner sends someone into my squeeze
page and they join my launch list, for every person that goes through that, it's going to
be worth a hundred dollars in sales and they're going to make $50 in commissions. That
can be super, super attractive.
!
At the end of the day, if you're looking for partners or if you're spending money on
advertising, the better your conversion which is driven by your sideway sales letter, the
more money you could spend on advertising or partners, it just makes the whole
business a lot easier.
!
That's why the sales letter or the sideway sales letter, is strategically so important and
that's why this launch list is so important because once you get people onto a launch
list and take them to that sequence, they're incredibly valuable.
!
Because of that value, it gives you a huge amount of flexibility within your business.
This is a little bit advance, sort of on that super business building, business launch
formula. I just wanted to get you exposed to this because making your math work right
gives you huge power on the long term in your business.
